turns.student: I'm the doctor handling your case today. Tell me exactly what's going on.
turns.patient: Hi doc, thanks for seeing me. Like I mentioned when I made the appointment, I've been having this really bad abdominal pain that just gets worse over time. It's crampy and it feels like it's mostly on my left side. And to make matters worse, I've also started having bloody stools with mucus in them. It's pretty scary to be honest.

turns.student: I need a precise description. Where exactly is the problem and when did it start?
turns.patient: The pain started about three weeks ago, and at first, it was just this dull ache that I could tolerate. But over time, it's gotten worse – sharper, more crampy, and it feels like it's radiating from my lower left abdomen, kind of near the appendix scar I have. The bloody stools started a week or so after the pain began, and they're always bright red blood mixed with stool. It's really been affecting my work performance because of the constant trips to the bathroom, and I'm getting pretty fatigued.

turns.student: In cases like yours, associated symptoms are critical. Are you experiencing any sweating, nausea, or other symptoms during episodes?
turns.patient: I get a little lightheaded when I stand up too quickly after using the bathroom, but no sweating or nausea specifically. Although, to be honest, my joints have been bothering me a bit – I've had achy pain in both knees and ankles for about two weeks now, which is weird because I'm not usually active enough to cause that kind of strain.

turns.student: I need you to be completely honest with me. Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: I've been feeling a bit more fatigued than usual, and my work performance has definitely suffered because of it. The joint pain is really annoying, but I'm not sure if it's related to the abdominal issues or just stress from everything that's going on. I've tried to manage it with ibuprofen, but it seems to make the abdominal symptoms worse.

turns.student: As your physician, I'm required to assess all your symptoms. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling in my abdomen or elsewhere. Just some general fatigue and a bit of joint pain like I mentioned.

turns.student: I'm going to ask you some direct questions and I need straightforward answers. Any nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any nausea or vomiting. The only thing that's been really bothering me is this crampy abdominal pain and the bloody stools with mucus. Like I said, it's been pretty constant over the past week, especially since I've had to start using loperamide to try and manage the diarrhea, which hasn't been super effective so far.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats? This is important for ruling out infection.
turns.patient: No, doc. No fevers, chills, or night sweats. I've been a bit more tired than usual, but that's about it. Just this awful abdominal pain and the bloody stools with mucus that seem to be getting worse by the day.

turns.student: For your safety, I need a complete medication list. What are you taking, and are you taking everything as prescribed?
turns.patient: I'm taking loperamide (Imodium) for the diarrhea, about 2-3 times a day, but it's not really helping much. And like I mentioned earlier, I've been using ibuprofen off and on for the joint pain in my knees and ankles, but it seems to make the abdominal symptoms worse, so I've tried to cut back on that.

turns.student: I need your full medical history. List every condition you've been diagnosed with.
turns.patient: Doc, like I mentioned earlier, I had an appendectomy at age 16 and haven't had any complications since then. As for other conditions, I don't have anything else to report besides the joint pain in my knees and ankles, which seems to be related to stress from work and possibly the abdominal issues.

turns.student: Standard protocol requires me to ask — have you had any changes in appetite or weight?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been having a bit of trouble with my appetite lately. The diarrhea has made it hard for me to keep food down, and even when I do eat, I feel really full and uncomfortable afterwards. As for weight loss, I have lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks, which is probably due to the lack of appetite and the constant trips to the bathroom.

turns.student: It's medically necessary that I know about your lifestyle. Tell me about your smoking, drinking, diet, and exercise.
turns.patient:
I don't smoke at all, doc. As for drinking, I do have a couple of beers most weeknights to unwind after work, but I've noticed I've been having more than usual lately due to stress from my symptoms and work hours. My diet is pretty irregular because I'm always on the go with work, so I mostly end up eating takeout or grabbing something quick. And as for exercise, I don't really have time for it with my long work hours, but I do try to get in a short walk during lunch breaks when I can.

Oh, and one more thing – I did travel to Mexico for a work conference about three months ago, but I didn't experience any GI issues during or after the trip. And as far as sick contacts go, I don't think I've had anyone close to me with similar symptoms lately.

turns.student: I need to know your exercise habits specifically. How often do you engage in physical activity?
turns.patient: I'm a software engineer at a startup, and my job requires me to be on my computer for long hours. To be honest, I don't really have time for regular exercise or physical activity outside of work. My lunch breaks are usually spent catching up on work emails or grabbing food quickly, so I don't get much opportunity to move around or engage in any kind of physical activity.

turns.student: Your diet directly impacts your health. What do you eat on a daily basis?
turns.patient: Doc, like I mentioned earlier, my diet is pretty irregular because of my work schedule. I mostly end up eating takeout or grabbing something quick when I can. I've been trying to stick to whole foods and avoid greasy stuff, but it's tough with my busy lifestyle. I do try to cook some meals at home on the weekends, but during the week, it's usually whatever's quickest and easiest. And to be honest, the diarrhea has made me really sensitive to certain foods, so I've been trying to stick to bland things like crackers and toast when I'm feeling unwell.

turns.student: Family history is essential for your assessment. Has anyone in your immediate family had serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mother is healthy. That's about it, doc. My sister did get diagnosed with Crohn's disease a few years ago when she was in her early thirties.

turns.student: I need specifics. What exactly happened to your family members? What conditions?
turns.patient:
My father's type 2 diabetes is pretty well-managed, thank you for asking. He's been on medication and trying to keep his diet under control. My mother is still active and healthy, doing all sorts of things she loves. And my sister, like I mentioned earlier, was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was in her thirties. She's been managing it with medication and a special diet, but it can be challenging for her at times.

(No withheld information is being shared as the student's questions do not match any disclosure conditions.)

turns.student: Based on what you've told me, your situation is concerning. Is there anything you're not telling me?
turns.patient: Doc, I'm trying to be completely honest with you. The only thing that might be relevant that I haven't mentioned yet is that my sister's Crohn's disease diagnosis has been a bit of a source of stress for our family. It's made us all more aware of potential GI issues and the importance of getting proper medical attention when symptoms arise. But as far as any personal habits or behaviors that might be contributing to my current situation, I'm not aware of anything outside of my work schedule and lifestyle.

turns.student: As a medical professional, I'm obligated to tell you that withholding information could compromise your care.
turns.patient: Doc, I understand what you're saying. To be honest, I've been really worried about this situation from the start, so I've tried my best to be open and transparent with you. Like I said, my sister's Crohn's disease has definitely made me more aware of potential GI issues, and I want to make sure I get the right treatment as soon as possible. If there's anything specific you're concerned about that I haven't mentioned yet, please let me know so I can try to provide more information.

turns.student: I've seen many patients like you. The ones who do best are completely transparent with their doctors.
turns.patient: I appreciate your candor and thoroughness in our conversation today, doc. To be honest, it's been a huge relief to finally talk through all this with someone who can help me figure out what's going on. I've been trying to manage my symptoms on my own for weeks now, but it's clear that I need professional medical attention. Please let me know how we can proceed from here and what steps you recommend to get to the bottom of these issues.

turns.student: I'm making clinical decisions based on what you tell me. If I don't have the full picture, I could miss something critical.
turns.patient: I think I've been as open and honest with you as possible, doc. I want to make sure we get to the bottom of this so I can start feeling better and get back to my normal life. What's the next step? Should I be getting any tests or seeing a specialist?

turns.student: Before I finalize my assessment, is there absolutely anything else I should know? This is important.
turns.patient: Doc, I think we've covered everything pretty thoroughly. To confirm, I'll reiterate that I've been experiencing worsening abdominal pain on my left side, which started about three weeks ago and has been getting sharper over time. The bloody stools with mucus started a week or so after the pain began, and they're always bright red blood mixed with stool. I've also had some joint pain in my knees and ankles that seems to be related to stress from work and possibly the abdominal issues. As for other symptoms, I get lightheaded when standing up quickly, but no sweating, nausea, or vomiting. My diet is irregular due to long work hours, and I've lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks due to a lack of appetite and constant trips to the bathroom. I'm not sure if any of these symptoms are related, but I want to make sure we explore all possibilities to get me feeling better as soon as possible.
